title: 控方证人
summary:
After being disturbed by the sound of a meowing cat outside his door at night, Dr. Castells observes a gray cat creeping across the lawn the following morning. He notes the unusual sight of the cat moving through a flock of birds without frightening them. During breakfast, Dr. Castells mentions the cat to Lady Cammichael, who reacts with heavy breathing and an unfriendly tone, insisting she has never owned a cat.

As Dr. Castells continues his examination of Arthur Carmichael, he notes that while the man appears blank and submissive, he is actually observing everything. Arthur shows a strong emotional dependence on Lady Cammichael while ignoring Miss Patterson. Seeking clarity regarding the cat, Dr. Castells asks the butler, who reveals that Lady Cammichael once owned a beautiful gray Persian cat that died about a week ago and was buried under a tree with purple leaves.

Dr. Castells discusses his findings with Sattler, who admits to hearing the cat but not seeing it, suggesting the voice sounded like a threat. During dinner in the green drawing room, the meowing returns, sounding angry and threatening, accompanied by the sound of fierce scratching on a copper door handle. When Sattler opens the door, nothing is there. Lady Cammichael and Miss Patterson are visibly terrified, while Arthur remains calm against his stepmother's knee. Miss Patterson seeks reassurance from Dr. Castells, who believes the threat is not directed at her. Later that night, Dr. Castells is startled awake by the sound of something being fiercely torn and dragged.
